title: Performance Basics and Common Issues
description: Understand what makes React Native apps slow and how to measure it.

slides.title: Why Performance Matters ⚡
slides.content:
# Smooth Apps = Happy Users

Users expect 60 frames per second (fps) for smooth interactions. If your app drops frames, it feels janky.

Common performance issues in React Native:
- Unnecessary re‑renders
- Large lists without virtualisation
- Heavy images
- Blocking the JavaScript thread
- Memory leaks

slides.title: Measuring Performance
slides.content:
## How to Know If You Have a Problem

- **Perf Monitor**: Shake device → 'Show Perf Monitor' to see fps and JS thread usage.
- **console.time** / **console.timeEnd**: Measure synchronous operations.
- **React DevTools Profiler**: Record interactions and see render times.
- **Flipper**: Powerful toolkit for inspecting network, logs, and layout.

We'll explore these tools in the next lesson.

quizData.question: What is a common cause of jank in React Native apps?
quizData.explanation: Unnecessary re‑renders waste CPU and can cause dropped frames.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Always test on a real device, especially older ones. Simulators are often faster and hide performance issues.
